cit.basis: Flat 15% CIT (Decree No. 3 of 1955 as amended by Law No. 2 of 2008) on the foreign (non-GCC) corporate ownership share of profit; wholly Kuwaiti/GCC-owned entities are exempt.
cit.note: Administered by the Ministry of Finance. Zakat (1%, Law No. 46 of 2006) and NLST (2.5%, Law No. 19 of 2000) apply to Kuwaiti listed companies and are out of scope for this SME estimator.

wht.note: Kuwait has no general withholding tax, but payers must retain 5% of contract payments until the recipient presents a tax clearance certificate from the Ministry of Finance.

vat.note: Kuwait has not implemented VAT as of the target period, though it is a signatory to the GCC VAT Framework Agreement.

personal_income_tax.note: No personal income tax on employment income.

social_insurance.note: PIFSS social security applies to Kuwaiti nationals only (approx. 10.5% employee / 11.5% employer); expatriates are not covered.

effective_date_range.basis: Target ruleset/params year; jurisdiction fiscal calendars and transitional periods may differ.

rate_threshold_assumptions: Rates and thresholds are simplified for LedgerField estimates.
Ownership blend, Zakat/NLST for listed companies, contract retention, relief, filing, and sector-specific rules may alter outcomes.
Verify against official guidance and local advisor before filing or production use.
limitations: Not legal, tax, accounting, or filing advice.
Does not encode every deduction, exemption, treaty, WHT, payroll, or filing rule.
High-stakes use requires local validation against official forms and current law.
